instructions: Spark plugin for GPU-accelerated data processing using RAPIDS cuDF. Maven-based, Scala/Java with Python integration tests. Key modules: sql-plugin/, shuffle-plugin/, iceberg/, delta-lake/, tests/, integration_tests/. Version-specific shims at sql-plugin/src/main/spark{VERSION}/.

rules.rule: GPU resources must use withResource() or closeOnExcept() from Arm.scala. Never use bare .close() on GPU resources.

GPU memory-allocating code must be wrapped in withRetry/withRetryNoSplit from RmmRapidsRetryIterator. The retry function must be idempotent. Input should be SpillableColumnarBatch before entering retry.
Enforce the 2GB per-partition size limit in shuffle code. Flag unbounded buffer growth.
Avoid unnecessary host-device memory copies. Keep data on GPU between consecutive operations.
Null-check cuDF native objects and results from aggregations/joins that may be empty.
Check decimal precision/scale for overflow. Must match Apache Spark behavior for DECIMAL128.
Maintain TIMESTAMP_NTZ vs TIMESTAMP_LTZ distinction. Verify timezone conversions.
No nested locks without documented ordering. Review synchronization in shuffle writers and GPU semaphore acquisition.
New GPU operators must declare fallback behavior in GpuOverrides with a clear log message for unsupported types.
Spark API changes must be shimmed for all supported versions. Verify shim classes override all required methods.
Every shim-specific source file must have a spark-rapids-shim-json-lines comment block after the copyright header listing supported Spark versions.
Handle schema evolution, partition spec changes, metadata consistency, and merge-on-read delete files correctly.
Verify row-level change tracking, commit protocol adherence, and conflict resolution in merge/update/delete.
Tests must clean up GPU resources and SparkSession in afterAll/afterEach to prevent cascading failures.
No hardcoded Thread.sleep, order-dependent tests, or non-deterministic data without fixed seeds.

Code touching JNI calls (cudf/spark-rapids-jni), private API interfaces (spark-rapids-private), or hybrid execution paths (spark-rapids-hybrid/Gluten) may break from concurrent upstream SNAPSHOT changes. Review for assumptions about upstream behavior.
Verify dependency version alignment across all build profiles and shaded JAR consistency.
